subject: DevOps
title: Security in Code Reviews
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Security in Code Reviews.

hook.text: The PR looked fine — until the reviewer spotted a hardcoded API key on line 87. Disaster averted.

buildup.text: Code reviews already check logic and style. Adding a security lens catches issues that tools miss.

discovery.text: Check input validation, auth logic, error handling, and data exposure during every code review.

twist.text: Security checklists help. A short list of common flaws for your stack makes reviews more consistent.

climax.text: Pair reviews with scans. Tools catch pattern flaws. Humans catch logic flaws. Both are needed.

punchline.text: Every code review is a security review. Add a security lens.

quiz.question: Why are human code reviews important for security even with automated scanners?
options: Humans catch logic-based security flaws that pattern scanners miss
Automated scanners never find any real vulnerabilities
Code reviews are cheaper than buying scanning tools
